meta_title: Roofing in Fort Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Fort Lauderdale, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems for HVHZ. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates. Financing from $87/mo.
h1: Fort Lauderdale's Most Trusted Roofing Contractor — Built for South Florida's Toughest Storms
intro:
Fort Lauderdale is one of the most beautifully positioned cities in all of South Florida — a coastal jewel where the Intracoastal Waterway winds through neighborhoods like Rio Vista, Victoria Park, and Las Olas, and where homes routinely command values around $480,000 or more. But that same waterfront proximity and tropical climate that makes Fort Lauderdale so desirable also makes your roof one of the most critical investments you'll ever make. With the Atlantic Ocean less than a mile from many residential streets, salt-laden air, intense UV exposure, and the ever-present threat of major hurricanes, a roof here doesn't just shelter your family — it's the first line of defense for everything you've built.

Nearly half of all homes in Fort Lauderdale were built before 1994, which means a significant portion of the city's housing stock was constructed before modern hurricane codes were enacted in the wake of Hurricane Andrew. From the charming bungalows of Sailboat Bend to the mid-century ranch homes of Tarpon River and the upscale canal-front properties near Flagler Village, aging roofing systems are quietly approaching the end of their service lives — often with homeowners unaware of the risk exposure they carry. An outdated or deteriorating roof in Broward County isn't just a maintenance issue; it's a financial liability that can affect your insurance rates, your home's resale value, and your family's safety when the next major storm makes landfall.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in full roof replacements and targeted repairs designed specifically for the demands of South Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone. We've completed roofing projects across Fort Lauderdale's most recognizable neighborhoods — from tree-lined streets near Las Olas Boulevard to homes just blocks from Fort Lauderdale Beach. Whether your current roof is showing signs of wear, you've recently experienced storm damage, or you're simply ready to upgrade to a system that performs at the highest level, our team brings the local expertise, certified products, and commitment to quality installation that Fort Lauderdale homeowners deserve.

section_why.h2: Why Fort Lauderdale Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties by doing one thing consistently well: delivering elite-level roofing work without ever cutting corners. Unlike many contractors who subcontract labor or rotate crews, every single Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is performed 100% in-house by our own trained, licensed technicians. Our team carries over 60 years of combined experience in South Florida roofing, which means we've seen how homes in Fort Lauderdale actually perform under real storm conditions — not just how they look on a specification sheet.

We hold an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and have successfully completed more than 500 residential roofing and window projects throughout the tri-county area. For Fort Lauderdale homeowners, that track record matters enormously. When you're investing in a roof for a home valued near or above $480,000, you can't afford to gamble on an unlicensed fly-by-night crew or a contractor who disappears after the check is cashed. Every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing project begins with a thorough inspection and consultation, includes full permit procurement through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, and is backed by the manufacturer warranties of the brands we install.

Our deep familiarity with Broward County's permitting processes and the specific requirements of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone means we navigate inspections and compliance without delays. We know what the inspectors look for. We know how the wind uplift patterns behave in coastal neighborhoods like Victoria Park and Rio Vista. We know which underlayment systems, fastener schedules, and installation techniques hold up when a storm like Wilma or Irma rolls through. That knowledge, combined with our commitment to staying on every job from start to final inspection, is what separates Premier Impact Windows & Roofing from the competition — and why our clients refer us to their neighbors.

Financing is another area where we've worked hard to eliminate barriers. With options starting at just $87 per month and $0 down programs available, protecting your Fort Lauderdale home with a premium new roof doesn't have to mean a painful upfront expenditure. We believe every homeowner deserves a safe, code-compliant roof — and we've structured our financing to make that goal achievable for nearly any budget.

section_history.h2: Fort Lauderdale's Storm History: Why Your Roof Cannot Afford to Be Average
section_history.body:
Fort Lauderdale has faced more than 10 named storms since 1990, and the damage patterns from those events tell a consistent story: the roof is always the most vulnerable component of a home. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 fundamentally reshaped the way South Florida thinks about wind resistance, exposing catastrophic failures in tens of thousands of homes built under outdated codes throughout Broward County. While Andrew made its most devastating landfall to the south, Fort Lauderdale was not spared — and the structural lessons learned from Andrew directly led to the stricter building standards that govern new construction and re-roofing projects today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought some of the most widespread roofing damage in Fort Lauderdale's recorded history. Wilma crossed South Florida as a powerful Category 3 storm with sustained winds that devastated neighborhoods from Plantation to the Riverwalk area. Thousands of homes lost tile, shingles, and in some cases entire roof sections. Many Fort Lauderdale homeowners discovered — too late — that older systems installed before the post-Andrew code revisions simply couldn't withstand the uplift pressures Wilma generated. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced that lesson again, damaging roofs across Broward County and sending insurance claims through the roof, so to speak, particularly in coastal communities near Fort Lauderdale Beach and the Intracoastal.

With the Atlantic hurricane season growing increasingly active and the National Hurricane Center consistently designating Fort Lauderdale as a high-risk coastal target, the time to upgrade an aging or underperforming roof is before a storm appears on the radar — not after. A hurricane-rated roof system installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing isn't just protection; it's peace of mind during every storm season for decades to come.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & HVHZ Requirements for Roofing in Fort Lauderdale
section_code.body:
Fort Lauderdale falls entirely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone — the most stringent wind-resistance designation in the entire state of Florida, and arguably in the entire United States. This classification, which covers Broward and Miami-Dade counties, imposes a level of building code compliance far more demanding than what's required in most of the country. For roofing projects, the HVHZ designation means that every material used, every fastener driven, and every layer of underlayment installed must meet specific Florida Product Approval standards and be tested to withstand winds of up to 170 miles per hour.

Under the current Florida Building Code, any re-roofing project in Fort Lauderdale must comply with Chapter 15 (Roof Assemblies) along with the specific amendments codified in the Florida Building Code's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone provisions. This includes mandatory use of approved secondary water barriers, specific fastener patterns for both shingles and tile systems, and proper flashing at all penetrations and transitions. The roofing system as a whole — including the deck attachment, underlayment, and surface material — must carry valid Florida Product Approval (NOA) documentation issued by Miami-Dade County or the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation.

All permits for roofing work in Fort Lauderdale are issued through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, and inspections are conducted at multiple stages of the project — including deck inspection before any new material is applied. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting and inspection process on your behalf. Our crews are thoroughly trained in HVHZ installation protocols, and we never schedule a final inspection until we are confident the work will pass on the first visit. For homeowners in Las Olas, Victoria Park, or anywhere else in the 33301 through 33394 zip code range, compliance isn't optional — and with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, it's never an afterthought.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series Shingles & West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Fort Lauderdale
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected roofing product lines available in South Florida: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product families carry the Florida Product Approvals necessary for installation in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, and both are engineered to deliver decades of reliable performance under the specific stress conditions that Fort Lauderdale's climate creates — including relentless UV radiation, salt air corrosion, heavy rainfall, and extreme wind uplift.

CertainTeed's Landmark series architectural shingles represent a significant upgrade over standard three-tab shingles in both aesthetics and performance. The dual-layer construction provides enhanced wind resistance and a dimensional, textured appearance that dramatically improves curb appeal — a meaningful consideration for homes in neighborhoods like Rio Vista or Victoria Park where presentation matters and HOA standards are common. CertainTeed Landmark shingles are rated for wind speeds consistent with South Florida's demanding conditions and carry SureStart Plus warranty coverage that protects homeowners against manufacturing defects and algae-related discoloration. For Fort Lauderdale's many tree-lined streets and humid microenvironments, the algae-resistant granule technology is a particularly practical feature.

For homeowners seeking the tile aesthetic that defines so many of Fort Lauderdale's Mediterranean-style and Spanish Colonial homes, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems deliver unmatched durability and visual authenticity. West Lake tiles are manufactured to exceed the wind uplift and impact resistance requirements of the HVHZ, and they're available in both concrete and genuine clay formulations to suit different architectural styles and budget considerations. Properly installed tile roofs in South Florida regularly last 40 to 50 years with minimal maintenance — making them one of the best long-term roofing investments available to Fort Lauderdale homeowners. Our crews are certified in West Lake's approved installation methods, ensuring that every tile is set to manufacturer specification and fully compliant with Broward County code.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Fort Lauderdale — and How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in Fort Lauderdale reflect the realities of the local market: HVHZ compliance requirements, higher-grade materials, and the technical expertise required for coastal installation all factor into the investment. For a full roof replacement on a typical Fort Lauderdale home, costs generally range from $12,000 to $30,000 or more depending on the size of the roof, the pitch, the materials selected, and any necessary deck repairs uncovered during installation. Given that the average Fort Lauderdale home is valued near $480,000, a properly installed hurricane-rated roof represents a relatively modest percentage of total home value — while protecting the entire asset from catastrophic loss.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options designed with South Florida homeowners in mind. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. That means a homeowner in Flagler Village or Tarpon River can get a fully code-compliant, hurricane-rated CertainTeed or West Lake roof installed today without waiting months to accumulate the capital. Interest-free promotional periods are often available, and our team will help you identify the financing structure that best matches your timeline and monthly budget.

From a pure return-on-investment standpoint, a new roof in Fort Lauderdale pays dividends that go well beyond storm protection. Real estate professionals consistently cite roofing condition as one of the top three factors that affect residential resale value in Broward County. A new roof can add immediate equity to your home, accelerate time-to-sale, and — as detailed in the next section — generate meaningful annual savings on your homeowner's insurance premium through wind mitigation credits. When you account for all of these factors together, a new Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof is among the most financially sound home improvement decisions a Fort Lauderdale property owner can make.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Fort Lauderdale Homeowners
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County is among the most expensive in the nation, and for good reason: Fort Lauderdale's coastal location, storm exposure, and HVHZ designation make it a high-risk market for insurers. Premiums for many Fort Lauderdale homes in the $400,000 to $600,000 range routinely run between $4,000 and $8,000 per year — sometimes significantly higher for older homes with roofs that don't meet current code. The good news is that a qualifying new roof replacement can generate substantial and immediate insurance savings that offset a significant portion of your installation investment over time.

Florida law requires insurance carriers to provide wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who can document qualifying features on their roofs. These credits are determined through a formal Wind Mitigation Inspection, during which a licensed inspector evaluates elements like roof shape, roof deck attachment, secondary water resistance, and the method of roof-to-wall connection. When your new Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof is installed to full HVHZ specifications — including the approved secondary water barrier, the correct fastener pattern, and the Florida Product Approved materials we use on every job — your home will typically qualify for the maximum available credits in multiple categories. For many Fort Lauderdale homeowners, this translates to annual insurance savings of $1,000 to $3,000 or more, depending on carrier and coverage level.

Major carriers writing policies in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several private market insurers, all recognize qualifying roofing systems in their wind mitigation discount structures. Some carriers have also begun requiring homes with roofs over 15 to 20 years old to undergo inspection or face non-renewal — making a proactive roof replacement not just financially beneficial but potentially necessary for maintaining continuous coverage. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will help you understand exactly how your new roof will be documented for wind mitigation purposes, so you can take full advantage of every available insurance benefit.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Fort Lauderdale home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on the age, overall condition, and extent of damage to your current roofing system. Roofs in Fort Lauderdale that are 15 to 20 years or older, show widespread granule loss, have multiple areas of damaged or missing tiles, or carry underlayment that has degraded past its useful life are typically better candidates for full replacement than repair. A repair on a failing roof can be a temporary and costly fix. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers thorough inspections to give you an honest assessment — we'll always recommend the approach that makes the most sense for your home's condition and your budget.

faqs.q: What does a new roof cost in Fort Lauderdale, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: A full roof replacement in Fort Lauderdale typically ranges from $12,000 to $30,000 or more depending on the roof's size, slope, material selection, and deck condition. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We work with you to find the right payment structure so you can move forward with a hurricane-rated, code-compliant roof without the stress of a large upfront payment. Contact us for a free, no-obligation estimate and we'll walk through your financing options in detail.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a roof replacement in Fort Lauderdale, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf. All roofing replacements in Fort Lauderdale require a permit issued through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. Because Fort Lauderdale is in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, all materials must carry valid Florida Product Approvals, and inspections are required at multiple stages including deck inspection and final. Our team handles permit applications, coordinates inspections, and ensures your project is fully closed out with the city — so you never have to worry about compliance or open permits affecting your home's title.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Fort Lauderdale, and will it disrupt my daily life?
faqs.a: Most standard residential roof replacements in Fort Lauderdale are completed within one to three days, depending on the size of the home, the materials being installed, and any deck repairs required. Our in-house crews are focused, organized, and respectful of your property throughout the process. We protect landscaping and outdoor living areas, clean up debris daily, and keep you informed at every stage. Permit processing timelines vary but we work to minimize delays and keep your project moving on schedule.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new roof installed to HVHZ standards qualifies your home for wind mitigation credits under Florida law, which carriers are required to apply to your premium. Depending on your current policy, carrier, and the specific features of your new roof, annual savings of $1,000 to $3,000 or more are achievable. Additionally, some insurers have begun non-renewing policies on homes with older roofs, so a new installation can also protect your coverage continuity. We document our work to facilitate your wind mitigation inspection and help you maximize every available discount.
